subject: Geography
title: Waitomo Glowworm Caves
description: A quick win: understand Waitomo Glowworm Caves.

hook.text: New Zealand's Waitomo Caves glow blue-green, lit by millions of tiny living creatures.

buildup.text: Arachnocampa luminosa are glowworm larvae that dangle sticky silk threads from the ceiling.

discovery.text: Their bioluminescent glow lures insects into the threads—a living chandelier that hunts.

twist.text: The glow disappears if the worms are disturbed, so tourists must stay silent in the caves.

climax.text: A cave ceiling lit by predatory worms looks like a starry sky—beautiful and lethal.

punchline.text: The prettiest lights in the cave are traps.

quiz.question: Why do Waitomo glowworms produce light?
options: To lure insects into their sticky silk threads
